relevantStudies.title: Long-term Outcomes of Combined Hypertension and Diabetes Management
relevantStudies.abstract: This meta-analysis examines the long-term cardiovascular outcomes in patients with comorbid hypertension and diabetes managed with intensive therapy protocols.
keyFindings: Intensive blood pressure control reduces cardiovascular events by 25%
Combined management protocols show 30% improvement in outcomes

relevantStudies.title: New Advances in Type 2 Diabetes Pharmacotherapy
relevantStudies.abstract: Review of novel pharmacological approaches to Type 2 Diabetes management including SGLT-2 inhibitors and GLP-1 agonists.
keyFindings: SGLT-2 inhibitors show cardiovascular benefits beyond glucose control
GLP-1 agonists demonstrate significant weight loss effects

clinicalGuidelines.title: 2025 ACC/AHA Guideline for the Prevention, Detection, Evaluation, and Management of High Blood Pressure in Adults
clinicalGuidelines.organization: American College of Cardiology/American Heart Association
keyRecommendations: Target blood pressure <130/80 mmHg for patients with diabetes
First-line therapy includes ACE inhibitors or ARBs for diabetic patients

evidenceSynthesis.hypertensionManagement: Current evidence supports targeting systolic blood pressure <130 mmHg in diabetic patients. ACE inhibitors like Lisinopril are preferred first-line agents due to renal protective effects.
evidenceSynthesis.diabetesManagement: Metformin remains first-line therapy. Recent evidence suggests SGLT-2 inhibitors may provide additional cardiovascular benefits for patients with established cardiovascular disease.
evidenceSynthesis.combinationRisk: Patients with both hypertension and diabetes have 2-3x higher risk of cardiovascular events. Integrated management approaches are essential.
